Purpose: Photo shoot prop + guide botany illustration — seeds in-frame identification
Primary Notes: Stratified seeds ready to germinate spring 2026; 70-80% germ rate. Ship USPS Priority. Order note: 'ship ASAP'.
Secondary Notes: Larger quantity; 1/4 lb min order. Good for close-up seed photography — stratified seeds photograph well.
Tertiary Notes: Note: Strictly Medicinal ginseng seed typically sold out by spring; seed is fall crop. Verify stock May 10 before ordering. If unavailable use dried root prop only.
Spring Availability Risk: CRITICAL — ginseng is a fall crop; spring stratified inventory is limited. Wisconsin Grown Ginseng and Dairyland are the reliable spring sources (stratified stock available). Verify availability May 10 and order same day.
Backup If Unavailable: Dried whole ginseng root (purchased locally at Whole Foods or Mountain Rose Herbs 2-day ship ~$15) is the stronger identification prop. Skip seeds and use root if spring stock exhausted.

Purpose: Identification photography — root is primary visual ID for guide interior
Primary Notes: Certified organic; ships USPS Priority 2-3 days. Dried whole root available year-round — no availability risk.
Secondary Notes: Purchase locally May 11 at nearest Whole Foods, co-op, or herb shop. Call ahead to confirm stock.
Tertiary Notes: Bulk organic; ships standard shipping. Adequate as tertiary.
Spring Availability Risk: NONE — dried root available year-round at retail and online
Backup If Unavailable: No backup needed — dried root is always available retail. This is the primary identification prop for the guide.

Purpose: Photo shoot prop + seed identification panel in guide
Primary Notes: Certified Organically Grown; standard spring availability confirmed. Order note: 'ship ASAP'.
Secondary Notes: Spring availability typical; bare-root divisions also available. Check for seed vs. plug availability.
Tertiary Notes: Appalachian-sourced goldenseal seed when in stock. Check availability May 10.
Spring Availability Risk: LOW — spring-available from cultivated sources. Multiple vendors carry cultivated goldenseal.
Backup If Unavailable: Dried goldenseal root (bright yellow color; most identifiable feature) is available from Mountain Rose Herbs ($12-18) as same-week fallback. Root photograph is more instructive for ID guides than seeds.

Purpose: Identification photography — yellow root color is key visual ID
Primary Notes: Bright yellow dried root is the iconic ID feature. Order alongside or instead of seeds.
Secondary Notes: Standard organic goldenseal root; same quality.
Tertiary Notes: Co-ops and natural food stores typically carry goldenseal root.
Backup If Unavailable: No backup needed. Dried root is the highest-priority goldenseal prop.

Purpose: Photo shoot prop + seed ID panel
Primary Notes: Spring-available from cultivated sources. Order note: 'ship ASAP'.
Secondary Notes: Spring availability typical; seeds or 3-packs (ship within 1 week of order in spring).
Tertiary Notes: Order by May 10 to ensure May 18 delivery at standard lead time.
Spring Availability Risk: LOW — spring-available from multiple cultivated sources
Backup If Unavailable: Potted live plant from Strictly Medicinal (~$8-12; ships mid-March through July) is a photographic upgrade. Order live plant if seeds unavailable OR if budget allows.

Common Name: Black Cohosh (potted live plant — optional upgrade)
Purpose: Full-plant composition for guide cover and Etsy listing hero
Primary Notes: Ships live March through July. This is the recommended upgrade — a live plant in-frame is significantly more guide-worthy than seeds alone.
Secondary Notes: Native plant nurseries in Appalachian range (NC/WV/TN/VA) may have live black cohosh in May. Call local native plant nurseries.
Tertiary Notes: 3-packs ship when well-rooted; check lead time at order.
Spring Availability Risk: LOW — plants available May through July from Strictly Medicinal
Backup If Unavailable: If no live plant available: use iNaturalist CC-BY archive photo for full-plant shot. No schedule impact.

Purpose: Photo shoot prop — rhizome ID and seed panel; spring ephemeral timing critical
Primary Notes: Check for bare-root or potted spring availability. Spring ephemeral — rhizome is the correct prop (orange-red color is the ID feature).
Secondary Notes: Bloodroot seeds viable only 1 week post-collection; commercial spring seed very narrow window. Check availability May 10.
Tertiary Notes: Secondary native plant vendor; check for bloodroot rhizome availability.
Spring Availability Risk: HIGH — spring ephemeral with narrow commercial seed window. Rhizome availability from nurseries is more reliable than fresh seed in May.
Backup If Unavailable: If rhizome unavailable from all vendors: use BHL botanical illustration (Sanguinaria canadensis is in multiple 19th-century herbal plates — public domain, free). No schedule impact for guide photography.

Purpose: Photo shoot prop — bulb/bulblet ID panel and seed close-up
Primary Notes: Spring bulblet availability variable; call or email May 10 to confirm. Bulblets are the preferred prop (more recognizable than seeds).
Secondary Notes: EFN sold out as of early May 2026. Check daily for restock. 2-3 day ship if available.
Quantity Needed (shoot): Search: 'ramps bulbs Appalachian' or 'wild leek bulbs.' Small-scale Appalachian growers list on Etsy. Verify cultivated (not wild-harvested) before ordering.
Order Status: CRITICAL — EFN sold out. Prairie Moon bulblet availability needs confirmation May 10. Check daily.
column31: Fresh ramp leaves from specialty grocery or farmers market ($3-8) are the highest-value ramps prop — readers recognize leaves immediately. Check farmers market week of May 12. If all props unavailable: iNaturalist CC-BY spring archive photos. No schedule impact.

Purpose: Leaf identification photography — highest-value ramps visual
Primary Notes: Fresh ramp leaves available at co-ops, specialty grocers, and farmers markets in May. Call farmers market vendors week of May 12.
Spring Availability Risk: MEDIUM — seasonal (peak April-May); likely still available in second week of May in Appalachian markets. Check local co-op week of May 12.
Backup If Unavailable: If unavailable fresh: use iNaturalist CC-BY spring 2020-2025 ramps observations for leaf imagery. Free; immediate.

Species: Panax quinquefolius + Hydrastis canadensis + Actaea racemosa + Sanguinaria canadensis + Allium tricoccum
Common Name: All 5 species — iNaturalist CC-BY archive photos
Purpose: Primary guide interior photography — habitat views / flower / full-plant shots not achievable in studio
Primary Notes: CRITICAL filter: photo_license=cc-by ONLY. Default license is CC-BY-NC (prohibits commercial use). Attribution required: 'Photo: [Observer], iNaturalist, CC BY 4.0'. Log each selected photo: species / observer / URL / guide section.
Quantity Needed (shoot): 5-8 Research Grade CC-BY observations per species (25-40 total)
Backup If Unavailable: Log all selected observations in WORKLOG.md: species / observer name / iNaturalist URL / intended guide section / license confirmed CC-BY. Target 5 angles per species: full plant / leaf / flower-fruit / root / seed-pod.

Purpose: Heritage botanical illustration — guaranteed fallback; also strongest visual for traditional-use sections
Primary Notes: Pre-1928 material is public domain — no attribution required (citing BHL is good practice). Key source: 'Ginseng and Goldenseal Growers Handbook 1912' at biodiversitylibrary.org/item/116021 — has engraved illustrations of both species. Search Latin name in BHL main search.
Backup If Unavailable: Download highest resolution TIFF available. Log source URL and BHL item number in WORKLOG.md. These are the fallback images if botanical garden partnerships do not respond.

column31: Cash event: May 10-12. Breakdown: stratified ginseng seeds $18-35 / goldenseal seeds + root $34-50 / black cohosh seeds + optional plant $14-30 / bloodroot rhizome $8-20 / ramps bulblets + fresh leaves $7-23 / all prop supplies $0 (iNaturalist and BHL are free). Upper bound assumes all optional live plant upgrades purchased. Does not include print shop run ($15-20 for botanical illustration prints). Write to COGS at first Phase 2 sale.
